records.ety: [L. suffusus, p. p. of suffundere to overspread; sub under + fundere to pour. See Fuse to melt.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Suffused (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Suffusing.]

defs: To overspread, as with a fluid or tincture; to fill or cover, as with something fluid; as, eyes suffused with tears; cheeks suffused with blushes.
quotes: When purple light shall next suffuse the skies.
